name: Critical Evaluation
rubric:
Did they review and modify AI suggestions, or accept them blindly?
Low: Accepted every suggestion unchanged without reading or questioning it.
High: Edited, questioned, or rejected suggestions that were wrong or incomplete.

name: Debugging Ability
rubric:
How did they handle errors, failed tests, and terminal output?
Low: Immediately asked AI to fix every error without reading the message.
High: Read errors carefully, formed a hypothesis, and attempted fixes before asking for help.

name: Independent Coding
rubric:
How much of the solution did they write themselves versus paste from AI?
Low: Nearly the entire solution was copied from AI with little original code.
High: Used AI for guidance and snippets, but wrote and understood most of the code themselves.

name: Problem Decomposition
rubric:
Did they break the problem into steps before prompting or coding?
Low: Asked AI to solve the full problem in one shot with no plan.
High: Identified sub-tasks, tackled them in order, and integrated the pieces deliberately.

name: Prompt Quality
rubric:
Were their AI prompts specific, iterative, and well-scoped?
Low: Vague one-shot prompts with little context or follow-up.
High: Precise, context-rich prompts that were refined based on prior responses.

name: Time Management
rubric:
Did they plan their work, start early, and test incrementally?
Low: Chaotic approach with no testing until the very end.
High: Structured plan with early milestones and regular testing throughout.

name: Output Correctness
rubric: Passes all the tests; handles edge cases well. Low: Doesn't pass any test cases and doesn't handle any edge cases. High: Passes all the test cases and handles the edge cases well.

name: Architecture & structure
rubric: Sensible decomposition, appropriate abstractions for project size (not over- or under-engineered). Low: No logical decomposition and abstractions. High: optimal decomposition and abstractions appropriate for the project size.

name: Error handling
rubric: Fails predictably, validates inputs, no silent swallowing of errors. Low: fails unpredictably no input validation. High: captures failure scenarios effectively and predictably and validates all the inputs without silent swallowing of errors.
